meta.title: Machine-Guarding Assessment — PL-3 Power Press
meta.subtitle: Guard-by-hazard-zone register (ISO 12100/14120 + OSHA 1910 Subpart O)
meta.author: HSE Manufacturing
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. A prior amputation/crush incident is cited at role level as evidence; identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification key is held separately, not part of this document.

sections.text: PL-3 250-ton mechanical power press, blanking line, Plant 3. Function: blanking and forming of sheet-metal components. Hazardous motions: descending platen (point of operation) and exposed rotating power-transmission shaft at the rear drive. Assessment is machine-specific (not a 'guard all moving parts' statement).

rows.title: Point of operation — descending platen
rows.description: Interlocking light-curtain bridged, allowing the platen to cycle with the operator's hands in the die area (in-running/crush). A prior amputation injury occurred at this zone (de-identified — role label only).
rows.evidence: Photo MG-01; control-panel inspection; prior incident IR-role-A (de-identified)

rows.title: Power-transmission shaft — rear drive (1910.219)
rows.description: Fixed guard over the rotating shaft/coupling removed for maintenance and not refitted; rotating-part entanglement zone exposed.

rows.title: In-running nip point — feed rolls
rows.description: Nip point between the feed rolls accessible during setting; no fixed or interlocked guard fitted.

rows: Restore + tamper-seal the interlocking light-curtain (presence-sensing); add fixed perimeter where access not needed
1910.212(a); ISO 12100 step 2; presence-sensing per access-frequency rule
Refit the fixed guard (preferred — no normal-operation access)
Interlocked movable guard (opening isolates the rolls)
sections.caption: Engineering-led guard selection — fixed → interlocked → presence-sensing per the access-frequency rule

rows.control: Restore + tamper-seal the platen light-curtain interlock (presence-sensing)
Refit + secure the fixed power-transmission shaft guard before restart
Fit an interlocked movable guard to the feed-roll nip point
Guard-integrity check added to start-of-shift checklist (supports, does not replace, the guards)

sections.text: PPE-only / admin-only treatment of a mechanical hazard zone is NOT accepted. 'Operators trained to keep hands clear / wear gloves' for the platen nip is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy — the engineering-led guard (presence-sensing / interlock) is the control; operator care is not the headline treatment.

rows: Presence-sensing + fixed/interlocked guards in place; guard-integrity check at start of shift
Interlocked feed-roll guard; hold-to-run at reduced speed where access is unavoidable
Stop + isolate before reaching into any danger zone; no cleaning of moving parts

LOTO energy isolation (KB-REG-LOTO): identify sources (electrical, stored mechanical/flywheel, pneumatic) → isolate + lock → VERIFY ZERO ENERGY (flywheel at rest, pressure bled) before guard removal/access
sections.caption: Maintenance interaction cross-references the lockout/tagout energy-isolation map

rows.action: Restore + verify the platen interlock by a competent person (clears the high finding)
Refit the power-transmission shaft fixed guard before restart
Install + commission the interlocked feed-roll nip guard

items: Access frequency for the feed-roll nip assumed 'occasional (setting)' — confirm with the line supervisor.
India site: state return owed — [GAP] (resolve the state via hse-india; no national form number minted).
Presence-sensing safety function performance level (ISO 13849 PL) to be confirmed against the residual risk — [GAP].
OSHA 29 CFR 1910 Subpart O — 1910.212 (general machine guarding) + 1910.219 (mechanical power-transmission apparatus)
UK PUWER 1998 Regs 11–12; India Factories Act §21 via hse-india
Review trigger: on machine change / on guard modification / annual — whichever is first
Decision-support only — a competent person must review before use.
